references.title: Identifying university students’ online academic help-seeking patterns and their role in Internet self-efficacy

extracted.Courses: Diverse Majors in Science, Humanities and Social Sciences
extracted.Features: [Infoormation Searching, Formal query, Informal query, Usage, Sharing, Communication, Verifying, Metacognition, Application, Learning]
extracted.Preprocessing Details: OAHS questionnaire is a 5-point Likert Scale, ISE is also a 5-point Likert Scale
extracted.Cluster Description:
Cluster 1 - Adaptive: More diverse experiences across OAHS experiences and higher confidence across ISE scales. 169 participants

Cluster 2 - Socially Afilliated: Higher scores on Formal and Informal Query than  Query than self-reliant. On ISE - more sharing. 134 participants

Cluster 3 - Self-Reliant: Better at Information Searching. Also, theses students have also shown higher confidence in ISE Usage.
extracted.Implications:
- System developers could set-up multiple help-seeking channels since different students have different habits

- Develop adaptive help-seeking detection and regulation mechanisms in computer environments.

- Organaize tasks and pedagogies across new learning paradigms (e.g. PBL) to form a trusted online learning community - something the authors argue is bvaluable.
extracted.Comments:
Authors do 2 surveys to 366 undergrads across different taiwanese institutions.

From the surveys the authors cluster the students into 3 distinct clusters using k-means.

Results show that there seem to be 3 main kinds of online help-seeking behavior and each has different preferences and psychological attributes.
extracted.Limitations:
- Only  considers Taiwanese Context

- Only uses surveys as source of information

- Only considers 3 OAHS patterns.Including more could probably lead to the creation of other categories

- There does not seem to be made a normalization for different fields - groups may be overly general.

annotations.text: The paper explores online help-seeking on a set of taiwanese HE students. Learners were asked to answer 2 surveys. Authors create 3 clusters - Adaptive, Socially-afiliated and self-reliant.
Clusters were later evaluated from the perspective of Internet Self-Efficacy.
